title: The Fork That Never Stopped Arguing

features.name: the split water
features.desc-on-touch: The creek divides here and never chooses. It runs east and west simultaneously, maintaining an argument with itself about which way is downstream.

features.name: the twin eucalypts in disagreement
features.desc-on-touch: Two trees grow from the fork point, each leaning the opposite direction. Their argument is ancient and patient. Neither has conceded. Neither will.

features.name: the path that holds both directions
features.desc-on-touch: Walkers stand here confused. The path splits but both directions somehow feel like the same path to two different places. Your footprint belongs to both destinations.

features.name: the stone that refuses to choose
features.desc-on-touch: A rock sits exactly at the fork point, worn by the creek's indecision into a smooth sphere. It has no weight. No force can move it because there is nowhere to push it that isn't also its home.

features.name: the bark notes carved in opposition
features.desc-on-touch: The eucalypts have been carved with notes from walkers—one side argues for east, the bark on the other side argues for west. The carvings have been accumulating for decades.

features.name: the echo that splits its own voice
features.desc-on-touch: Call here and your voice returns from both directions at once, each echo arguing back. The fork has learned to speak in disagreement.

inscription: the fork chose to never choose; and so it remains both ways at once, teaching walkers that some arguments are solved only by refusing to settle them

file.name: FollowBothPaths_GuideToContradiction.txt
file.note: A walker's attempt to follow both forks simultaneously by describing the fork-choice in three different tenses at once. The methodology works perfectly and makes no practical sense.

exits.label: back to where the merger still sings in harmony
down the eastern path that is also the western path
exits.to: NEW: down the eastern path that is also the western path

exits.label: down the western path that is also the eastern path
